name: New England Clam Chowder No.x
recipeInstructions:
------------
1. Add the scrubbed littleneck clams to 1 quart boiling water. Simmer until clams open. Remove and discard any clams that do not open.
2. Strain the cooking liquid through several layers of damp cheesecloth, reserving both the liquid and the clams (chop and reserve).
3. In a large heavy soup pot, render all fat from the diced bacon. Remove the crispy bits and blot them on paper towels. Discard the bacon bits.
4. Add 1 large onion to the hot fat in the soup pot and cook over moderate heat until softened.
5. Stir in 1 tablespoon flour and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
6. Add the reserved clam cooking liquid, diced potatoes, salt, and pepper. Cook until the potatoes are barely tender.
7. Stir in the chopped clams, reserving any additional bacon bits that floated to the surface.
8. Heat 3 cups of milk separately. Once heated, add it to the soup pot and stir gently.
9. For a Manhattan-style variation, substitute mussels for clams and add 2 cups chopped canned tomatoes, 1 cup vegetable juice cocktail, 1 diced seeded bell pepper, 1 bay leaf, and 1/4 teaspoon each dried oregano and basil as soon as the potatoes are cooked. Simmer an additional 5 minutes.
10. Omit milk for this variation.
